checks.moreThanSixLines: You used more than six lines of code.

createTwoFerFunction.name: Create two-fer function
createTwoFerFunction.description: Write a twoFer function that takes a name and returns 'One for [name], one for me.' If no name is given (empty string), use 'you' instead of the name.

solveInSixLines.name: Solve in six lines of code
solveInSixLines.description: Can you solve this with only six lines of code?

twoFerDefault.name: No name given
twoFerDefault.description: No name is given so return 'One for you, one for me.'

twoFerAlice.name: Name given as Alice
twoFerAlice.description: Her name is 'Alice' so return 'One for Alice, one for me.'

twoFerTom.name: Name given as Tom
twoFerTom.description: His name is 'Tom' so return 'One for Tom, one for me.'

bonus1.name: Six lines of code
bonus1.description: Solve the exercise with only six lines of code.

whatChanges.question: What changes based on the input?
whatChanges.answer: Just one thing. The name in the middle of the sentence. If you were given a name, use it. If not, use the default `"you"`.

checkGivenName.question: How do I check 'was I given a name'?
checkGivenName.answer: An if-statement comparing the input to the empty string `""`. If it's empty, use `"you"`. Otherwise, use the input.

buildSentence.question: How do I build the final sentence?
buildSentence.answer: Join the three pieces (`"One for "`, the chosen name, and `", one for me."`) using concatenation (`+`) or a template string.
